content:
Hi,
I'm currently learning how to work with Rust in the kernel and I was wondering I this integration support cross compiling for embedded devices like raspberry pi or BeagleBone dev boards.
The result that I'm aiming is create device drivers written in Rust integrated within a dev borad.

The kernel usual support for cross-compilation works for Rust. However, Rust support is only supported for a few (sub)architectures so far: https://docs.kernel.org/rust/arch-support.html (AArch64 and RISC-V coming soon).

Miguel Ojeda said:

The kernel usual support for cross-compilation works for Rust. However, Rust support is only supported for a few (sub)architectures so far: https://docs.kernel.org/rust/arch-support.html (AArch64 and RISC-V coming soon).

So no ARM so far?
